description_long: This object consists of two pieces of gunkan-maki sushi, each formed with a cylindrical base of white rice wrapped in a dark, textured sheet of nori seaweed. The tops are generously filled with bright orange-red fish roe (ikura), with the glossy, translucent spheres piled high and some spilling slightly over the edges. A few thin, green strips, likely scallion or chive, are tucked into the roe as garnish. The sushi pieces are presented on a rectangular wooden serving board with a natural grain and a smooth finish. The overall appearance is fresh, vibrant, and visually appealing, with a contrast between the dark nori, the vivid roe, and the light wood. The presentation suggests a high-quality sushi experience, suitable for a restaurant or upscale urban food setting.
description: Two pieces of gunkan-maki sushi with bright orange-red fish roe and green garnish, served on a rectangular wooden board. The sushi features dark nori wrapping and a visually striking, fresh presentation.
description_view_0: Front view showing both sushi pieces side by side, highlighting the glossy roe and green garnish on a wooden board.
description_view_1: Side view focusing on one sushi piece, with the nori wrap and roe mound clearly visible, and the board extending backward.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backs of both sushi pieces, with the nori wrap and roe visible, and the green garnish more prominent.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the other sushi piece, with the roe and nori wrap in focus and the wooden board receding.
